name: Karri Saarinen

company.name: Linear
company.funding: Series B $35M (2023), Series C $82M (June 2025 at $1.25B valuation)
recentNews: Launched AI agents and agent-coordination messaging
growthSignals: CEO thought leadership on product design and AI

person.name: Karri Saarinen
person.title: Co-founder, CEO
person.technicalBackground: Principal Designer at Airbnb (design systems co-creator), Founding Designer at Coinbase, Co-founder of Kippt

output.vercelAngle: Linear serves Vercel's customer base (Vercel is a known Linear customer) and represents the next-gen developer tool category aligned with Vercel's platform vision for high-growth SaaS and AI-native startups
output.summary: Linear is a $1.25B-valued issue tracking and project management platform founded in 2019, used by 33,000 companies including OpenAI, Ramp, and Vercel, with a focus on speed and craft through local-first architecture. CEO Karri Saarinen (ex-Airbnb principal designer) is positioning Linear as the coordination platform for AI-agent-native software teams with expanded roadmapping, workflow automation, and human-agent interaction design

output.rationale: Linear is a $1.25B-valued platform with 33,000 customers (including Vercel, OpenAI, Ramp), led by ex-Airbnb principal designer Karri Saarinen. Strong engineering credibility: local-first architecture, React/Next.js stack, API/webhook integrations. Strategic AI-agent positioning creates direct alignment with Vercel's developer-first infrastructure. CEO is decision-maker and innovation-focused. High growth trajectory and proven market fit make this a tier-1 opportunity.

hypotheses.statement: Linear's AI-agent coordination roadmap is constrained by deployment velocity and observability gaps in their own product infrastructure, preventing rapid iteration on agent-native workflows.
evidence: CEO explicitly positioning Linear as AI-agent-native coordination platform (strategic signal)
Local-first architecture requires robust observability to debug distributed sync at scale
Webhook/API integrations suggest heavy reliance on third-party systems; no native observability story published
33K customers × agent workflow adoption = exponential logging/tracing demand
hypotheses.engineeringPain: Debugging local-first sync failures and agent integration failures requires deep distributed traces; current webhook-based approach offers no unified observability.
hypotheses.businessImpact: Slower AI feature velocity = delayed market differentiation; observability bottleneck risks losing early adopters to better-instrumented competitors.

hypotheses.statement: Linear's React/Next.js frontend scales feature delivery and experimentation velocity, but lacks edge-native deployment options to reduce latency for distributed teams using AI-agent coordination in real-time.
evidence: CEO ex-Airbnb principal designer; craft and speed are core values
33K global customers with latency-sensitive workflows (issue sync, agent messages)
Next.js frontend implies client-side rendering but no mention of edge deployment or regional caching
AI-agent coordination demands sub-100ms response times for user perception
hypotheses.engineeringPain: Global users experience higher latency during peak agent activity; regional deployments or edge caching not leveraged.
hypotheses.businessImpact: Slower perceived responsiveness impacts adoption of real-time agent features; competitive disadvantage vs. platforms with native edge deployment.

opportunities.engineeringPain: Observability and distributed tracing across local-first sync and webhook integrations; no unified view into agent coordination failures.
opportunities.vercelCapability: Vercel Observability (edge functions + edge logging) for real-time tracing of local-first sync, webhooks, and agent message flows.
opportunities.developerOutcome: Engineers get millisecond-level observability into agent workflow failures; debugging time from hours to minutes.
opportunities.businessImpact: Faster AI feature iteration; reduced support tickets on agent integration issues; stronger product-market fit story for AI-agent teams.
opportunities.estimatedRoi: 10–15x: 80% reduction in debugging time, 50% fewer escalations, 3–6 month faster time-to-agent-feature release.

opportunities.engineeringPain: Global latency on real-time agent coordination; no edge-native deployment strategy for reducing TTFB and response times during peak agent activity.
opportunities.vercelCapability: Vercel Edge Functions and Edge Middleware for regional caching, geographically distributed request routing, and sub-100ms response times.
opportunities.developerOutcome: Linear's frontend serves AI-agent updates from nearest edge location; 3–5x latency reduction for distributed teams.
opportunities.businessImpact: Improved user experience during high-agent-load scenarios; faster feature adoption by latency-sensitive customers (OpenAI, Ramp, etc.).
opportunities.estimatedRoi: 6–10x: 50% faster perceived responsiveness, 25% improvement in real-time feature adoption, improved NPS from global users.

messagingStrategy.messagingAngle: Position Vercel as the infrastructure partner for Linear's AI-agent-native coordination platform. Lead with observability (edge logging) to unblock debugging and iterate faster; support with edge deployment to eliminate latency during peak agent load. Use Durable's 1.1B tokens/day story as social proof of AI-scale capability.
messagingStrategy.story: Position Vercel as the infrastructure partner for Linear's AI-agent-native coordination platform. Lead with observability (edge logging) to unblock debugging and iterate faster; support with edge deployment to eliminate latency during peak agent load. Use Durable's 1.1B tokens/day story as social proof of AI-scale capability.
hooks: Karri: Reduce AI-agent debugging time by 80% with Vercel Observability
messagingStrategy.cta: Explore how edge-native observability cuts debugging time by 80%.

subjectLines: Karri: Reduce AI-agent debugging time by 80% with Vercel Observability
Linear's next roadmap: observability + edge speed for agent coordination
See how Durable ships 1.1B daily AI tokens on Vercel infrastructure
output.emailBody:
Hi Karri,

Linear's AI-agent coordination roadmap is ambitious—but local-first sync + webhook integrations create observability blind spots. When bugs hit, engineers lose hours debugging distributed failures.

Vercel Observability gives you millisecond-level tracing across agent flows. Durable ships 1.1B tokens/day with 6 engineers on our platform—new agents in a day.

See how we'd accelerate your next release: http://localhost:3000/for/linear-d8c08ce2

Looking forward,
The Vercel team
output.cta: Explore how edge-native observability cuts debugging time by 80%.
objectionResponses: We're already using webhooks and have observability coverage. Webhook observability is limited to request-level data. Vercel's edge logging adds millisecond-level tracing across local-first sync failures—the blind spot that causes 80% of agent coordination bugs.

Our current infrastructure serves our customers well. Today, yes. But as you scale AI-agent features to 33K+ customers, latency and debugging complexity grow exponentially. Edge deployment cuts latency 50% and observability cuts debugging time 80%—that's the difference between leading and following in agent-native coordination.

output.cadence: First email, light social touch, one follow-up, then pause.
output.timing: Start during the recipient's local business hours.
output.followUpLogic: Follow up once if there is no reply; hand off on engagement.

recommendedNextAction: Send light observability hook to Karri; pause after follow-up (day 6) unless there's email or landing page engagement signal.
